subject: Design and Technology
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: GRAPHICS
topics: Think of this; you have been given assorted parts for a chair or table to assemble and each part
is unique. How easier would that be? Would you need something to use as a guide? Is it a
worded or pictorial guide? It is clear that a pictorial guide with annotations would be
appropriate in this case. It would help to communicate the steps needed when assembling the
activities: Natural: Well ventilated and lit area/space to avoid eye strain
Inquiry based: Allow learners to explore the questions and problems through
Project based: Assign learners real world drafting projects that require application of
assessment: Formative: Observation, checklist/worksheet, Q&A, Group discussion
Summary: Drafting aids can be used to come-up with various designs in a real-
world situation. Learners need to acquire relevant skills on how to use drafting aids.
This is achieved through a continuous manipulation of these drafting aids. When good skills
are gained, leaners will easily design the needed objects.
Learning activity 2: Applying varied effects to lettering work
